properties.name: Childress Powered Land (Hwy 287)
properties.power_notes: Multiple 345kV and 138kV lines traverse the area; grid power via the Tesla substation. Flat site, fully outside floodplain.
properties.status: Available immediately
properties.broker: CBRE (Brant Bernet, Chris Herrmann, Phoebe Bernet)

properties.name: South Dallas DC Development (Waxahachie)
properties.power_notes: 138kV and 345kV infrastructure upgrades approved by ERCOT (Oncor); pathway to hundreds of MW. Water agreement + TPDES sewer permit in place.
properties.status: Available immediately
properties.broker: CBRE (Brant Bernet, Chris Herrmann, Phoebe Bernet)

properties.name: West Texas Panhandle 5,000-Acre AI Campus
properties.power_notes: Three power lanes: 250MW behind-the-meter ramp from mid-2026, 250MW wind offtake from Jul 2026, high-pressure gas line supporting 500MW on-site generation in 12 months (ramp to 1GW). Own 345kV frontage; can loop into SPP.
properties.fiber_notes: Zayo and AT&T fiber backbone; municipal water access
properties.status: Active listing

properties.name: North Abilene Tract A (1,450± ac)
properties.power_notes: Between two major data centers under construction near Abilene (Stargate corridor).
properties.status: Active listing

properties.name: North Abilene Tract B (1,630± ac, on-site substation)
properties.power_notes: Existing electric substation and transmission lines in NW portion of property; between two data centers under construction.
properties.status: Active listing

properties.name: Pineywoods (855 ac, East Texas)
properties.power_notes: ~640 buildable acres served by three HV transmission lines (345kV, 138kV, 69kV) and five major interstate natural gas pipelines; supports front-of-meter and behind-the-meter.
properties.status: Active listing

properties.name: DFW 1GW Ground-Lease (4×250MW blocks)
properties.power_notes: 1GW ground-lease opportunity subdivided into four 250MW blocks scheduled to energize 2028.
properties.status: Active (ground lease)

properties.name: DFW Strategic 1,500-Acre Hyperscale Parcel
properties.power_notes: Positioned for hyperscale campus development; 345kV transmission frontage tracts in broker portfolio.
properties.status: Active listing

properties.name: 88-Acre Substation-Adjacent Site (Waco–Austin corridor)
properties.power_notes: Substation adjacency; outside city jurisdiction (ETJ advantages). Marketed for crypto/AI training workloads.
properties.status: Active listing

properties.name: West Texas 82-Acre Edge/Modular AI Site
properties.power_notes: Marketed for edge or modular AI deployment.
properties.status: Active listing

properties.name: Aquila Site (Hill County, 800+ ac)
properties.power_notes: Four contiguous parcels under contract at ~$100k/acre (~14× appraisal) for proposed data center development.
properties.status: Under contract / contested

properties.name: Amazon Data Services — Bastrop County (1,300+ ac acquired)
properties.power_notes: One of the largest Central Texas land assemblies tied to hyperscale development (May 2026).
properties.status: Acquired (market comp)

properties.name: Tract — Caldwell County Technology Park (1,515 ac, multi-GW)
properties.power_notes: Master-planned multi-gigawatt data center technology park between Austin and San Antonio.
properties.status: Acquired / in development

properties.name: CloudBurst — San Marcos (706 ac, 1.2GW approved)
properties.power_notes: 1.2GW campus approvals; ~$14.5B projected investment.
properties.status: Approved / in development

properties.name: APR Energy — Pampa (800 ac contracted + 500 ac ROFR)
properties.power_notes: On-site gas turbines planned; phase 1 targeted early 2026.
properties.status: Contracted / in development

properties.name: Advanced Energy & Intelligence Campus — Amarillo (aka Project Matador/HyperGrid, ~7,570 ac)
properties.power_notes: Fermi America / Texas Tech University System campus on a 99-year ground lease; ~6GW of natural gas generation already TCEQ-permitted (Nov 2025) en route to a targeted 17GW total (11GW natural gas, 4.4GW nuclear, plus solar/battery); 18M sq ft of data centers planned.
properties.status: In development (TCEQ preliminary approval for first 6GW clean natural gas facility, Nov 2025)

properties.name: Black Mountain Power — Bowie County (600 ac acquired)
properties.power_notes: Fort Worth company purchased ~600 ac (Mar 2026) for data center development.
properties.status: Acquired (market comp)

properties.name: Stargate (Crusoe/OpenAI) — Abilene anchor campus
properties.power_notes: Anchor hyperscale AI campus driving the Abilene powered-land corridor.
properties.status: Under construction

properties.name: Galaxy Helios — West Texas (1.6GW+, 1,500+ ac)
properties.power_notes: Former bitcoin mine converted to AI data center campus; grown to 1.6GW+ in 2026 — the template for West Texas powered-land conversions.
properties.status: Operating / expanding

properties.name: Stream Data Centers — Graham Project (ex-Plug Power, 164MW interconnect)
properties.power_notes: Plug Power sold its Graham, TX land and associated 164MW of grid interconnection assets to Stream Data Centers for up to $76.5M ($50M at closing, up to $26.5M tied to confirmed load capacity in the final Texas utility interconnection agreement).
properties.status: Sale agreement signed; closing expected on/about Jul 31, 2026

properties.name: Big Digital Energy / 10NetZero JV — Hood County Site
properties.power_notes: Powered industrial site with 17MW operational power today, expandable to 111MW grid power pending ERCOT validation. On-site natural gas pipelines (two 12-inch, one 20-inch) allow behind-the-meter generation for a buildout to up to 311MW total. 30,000+ sq ft of existing structures to be repurposed for AI data center use.
properties.status: 50/50 JV + signed letter of intent to acquire (Jul 6, 2026)

properties.name: MARA — Matagorda County Site (ex-HIF USA, up to 2GW)
properties.power_notes: Definitive agreement with HIF USA for a large-scale powered land site ~90 miles SW of Houston; access to up to 1GW of grid capacity by Oct 2027, up to 2GW by Apr 2028. To be developed with Starwood Digital Ventures for HPC/flexible compute including Bitcoin mining.
properties.status: Definitive agreement signed (Jul 2026)

properties.name: AmpZ Champion / EPG Champion — Angelina County (former Southland Paper Mill)
properties.power_notes: Memorandum of option for ~1,041 acres across multiple abstracts/surveys at the former Southland Paper Mill site; nearby land could expand the project to ~4,000 acres. Part of the site to be used as a sound/wetland buffer. Estimated $1.05B private capital investment if finalized.
properties.status: Option agreement / under development and community negotiation (Jul 2026)

properties.name: Tract — Medina Technology Park (265 ac, 500MW)
properties.power_notes: Shovel-ready data center campus off CR 4516/US-90W, served by Medina Electric Cooperative and East Medina County SUD; site energization targeted mid-2028; adjacent to an existing Microsoft campus in Castroville. One of three San Antonio-area Tract sites (with Caldwell County and a third location) totaling ~3,500 ac / 5,220MW across the region.
properties.status: Acquired / in development

properties.name: New Era Energy & Digital — TCDC Campus (Ector County, 438+54 ac, 1GW+)
properties.power_notes: Flagship hyperscale AI/HPC campus; 438 acres owned plus a 54-acre corridor pending; on-site natural gas-fired power generation with carbon capture (up to 250,000 metric tons CO2/yr); phased buildout toward 1+GW. Non-binding JV letter of intent with Stream Data Centers for development/financing announced Apr 2026.
properties.status: In development (JV LOI with Stream Data Centers, Apr 1 2026; underwriters' option funding secured Apr 14 2026)

properties.name: Infrakey — Lacy Lakeview Data District (520 ac, 925MW)
properties.power_notes: Public-private data center district formed with the City of Lacy Lakeview on farmland west of Elm Mott; includes a planned ~1.2GW gas-fired power plant; ~$10B projected investment. Facing active community opposition and an annexation/jurisdiction dispute with the City of Waco.
properties.status: Announced / in development; community and jurisdictional disputes ongoing as of Jun 2026

properties.name: Helena Harbor Industrial Park
properties.power_notes: 10-inch, 800 PSI steel natural gas line installed on-site (service established Jan 2015). Publicly owned, flood-protected industrial park behind the main Mississippi River levee at river mile 652 AHP, ~65 mi south of Memphis; direct barge docking on a 2.25-mile, 9-ft-deep slackwater harbor/turning basin; 60-ton crane on site.
properties.status: Available; container yard construction approved Dec 2024, additional rail/water supply improvements expected 2027

properties.name: Belwood Industrial Site
properties.power_notes: Entergy substation directly adjacent across River Terminal Road. Rail via Natchez Railway (connects to CN spur in Brookhaven). Adjacent to Natchez-Adams County Port (rail, liquid loading dock, 250-ton pedestal crane). ~1,500 ft Mississippi River frontage; protected by a Delta Regional Authority-funded 8,000-ft ring levee (~$1.27M). One source cites 214 acres for a related parcel; unresolved discrepancy with the 110-acre figure.
properties.status: County-owned; Adams County has granted a purchase option on part of the property to a prospective client (as of mid-2020s reporting) — reconfirm current availability

properties.name: Madison Parish Port — adjacent 500-acre tract
properties.power_notes: Electricity from Entergy Louisiana, natural gas from CenterPoint Energy (no specific substation voltage or pipeline tap size confirmed). Core 266-acre port site has a 400-ft concrete T-dock for barge loading/unloading and certified truck scales; a separate 35-acre fenced sub-parcel has a 10,000 sq ft warehouse plus 5 smaller buildings.
properties.status: Available for purchase

properties.name: West Memphis Rail Port Logistics Park + Port Terminal
properties.power_notes: "Multi-megawatt power supply" stated for the Rail Port Logistics Park (no specific MW figure); 2.7 MGD excess water / 1 MGD excess wastewater capacity. Adjacent Watco-operated port terminal (1,400 ac build-to-suit + 10 ac general purpose) has barge dock, 100-ton boom crane, heavy-haul road access, and a new ~13,500-ft heavy-haul rail lead to the St. Francis levee base; interchange with Union Pacific, BNSF, CSX, Canadian National, Norfolk Southern. No specific substation or gas-pipeline tap named in sources.
properties.status: Available; part of a 2025 West Memphis industrial-growth push
properties.broker: Watco (port terminal, sales@watco.com); City of West Memphis Economic Development (logistics park)

properties.name: Rosedale-Bolivar County Port
properties.power_notes: Port property totals 1,200 acres with 1,123 acres stated available (largest contiguous tract up to 500 ac); separately the adjoining 270-acre Rosedale Industrial Park (landside of the levee) has 200+ acres available. No substation or gas-pipeline detail found in sources — needs follow-up.
properties.status: Available; actively marketed by the port commission

properties.name: Ingevity Crossett (former GP Crossett pine-chemicals plant)
properties.power_notes: Oleochemical/tall-oil-rosin plant using natural-gas-fired hot oil heaters for product separation (explicit gas dependency; tap size/operator not stated in sources). Co-located within the large Georgia-Pacific Crossett industrial/mill complex, implying shared rail and water infrastructure — inference, not independently sourced.
properties.status: Production ceased August 2024 (~70 employees affected, operations relocated to North Charleston, SC); no sale/redevelopment news found yet

properties.name: Beale Infrastructure "Project Blue" Data Center (TEP-anchored)
properties.city: Tucson (unincorporated, SE Employment and Logistics Center)
properties.power_notes: 10-year Energy Supply Agreement with Tucson Electric Power (TEP) approved by the Arizona Corporation Commission 4-1 on Dec 3, 2025; TEP to provide 138kV service; customer pays full large-user rate, no discounts; developer responsible for dedicated infrastructure; load ramps over 18 months to full 286MW draw by 2028. Full build-out (~10 buildings, 2M sq ft) could reach up to 600MW.
properties.status: ESA approved by ACC (Dec 2025); Pima County land-use deal approved Dec 17, 2025; construction phase 1 targeted 2026-2029, online target May 2027

properties.name: Project Midway Data Center
properties.power_notes: Served by Electrical District No. 3 (ED3), not APS/SRP — the ~225MW need is described as nearly all of ED3's current capacity; on-site substation planned (sources disagree on substation footprint, 10 ac vs 50 ac); backup generators beside each of 5 planned data-center buildings; ~100MW proposed to come from the adjacent "Project Bella" 480MW-gas/440MW-battery facility sited directly south.
properties.status: Rezoning (GR to I-3 + PAD) approved by Pinal County Board of Supervisors 5-0, Nov 19, 2025

properties.name: Energy Generation & Technology Campus + Griffin Energy Project
properties.city: South of Maricopa (data-center campus); SE of Stanfield (power site)
properties.power_notes: Dedicated 2,685-acre power campus near Stanfield ("Griffin Energy Project," developer Copia Power) to feed the 495-acre data-center/tech campus south of Maricopa: up to 550MW solar, up to 550MW battery storage, and up to 500MW gas-fired generation. Combined project investment ~$9.5-10B.
properties.status: Comprehensive plan/rezoning approved by Pinal County Board of Supervisors, Nov 19, 2025

properties.name: Sandow Lakes / Xebec Megasite (former Alcoa aluminum plant)
properties.power_notes: ~1.5GW of existing ERCOT-connected capacity today, anchored by the legacy Alcoa electrical backbone that powered the former aluminum smelter; an additional 1.2GW Oncor transmission substation was expected to deliver capacity in Q3 2025, with further substations planned to reach ~3.9GW by 2028.
properties.status: Acquired by Xebec Holdings (SLR Property I LP) from Alcoa in Nov 2021 for $240M; Phase 1 (Advanced Manufacturing Logistix Campus, up to 35M sq ft) under construction, targeted ready-for-occupancy Q3 2026 — bulk of the 33,000-acre site remains available for additional build-to-suit development

properties.name: Madison County Mega Site (Entergy Qualified Site Program)
properties.power_notes: Mississippi PSC-approved $57M Entergy substation (in service ~Dec 2021) initially serves up to 80MW with capacity to provide up to 300MW in the future via three independent transmission sources; part of Entergy Mississippi's Qualified Site Program.
properties.status: Certified/qualified site; 765+ acres qualified (Madison County Economic Development Authority owns 325 ac, ~875 ac under option); near the Nissan Canton plant off I-55
properties.broker: Madison County Economic Development Authority / Entergy Mississippi

properties.name: Beauregard Airport Industrial Site
properties.power_notes: A natural gas pipeline runs across the site; dual-feed redundant electric service from Cleco and Beauregard Electric (Cleco's Western Louisiana region).
properties.status: LED-certified, shovel-ready; potable water on-site, city sewer nearby; Timber Rock rail spur connects to the Kansas City Southern main line through DeRidder; frontage on US Hwy 190
properties.broker: Louisiana Economic Development / Beauregard Economic Development

properties.name: International Paper Red River Containerboard Mill
properties.power_notes: Former containerboard mill (~800,000 tons/yr annualized capacity); adjoins the Natchitoches Parish Port, a multimodal industrial center on the Red River Waterway with barge, rail (4,000+ ft of Kansas City Southern Railroad frontage), and US Hwy 71 highway access. No specific gas-tap or substation figures published yet — flagging for follow-up.
properties.status: Closure announced Feb 13, 2025; operations ceased end of April 2025 (~674 jobs); Louisiana Economic Development stated it is working to locate a buyer to bring the site back into production

properties.name: Pima County Aerospace Research Campus (TEP transmission-served)
properties.power_notes: Tucson Electric Power is building a new 3-mile, 138kV transmission line linking its existing system to a proposed Franco Wash Switchyard to serve the campus; Arizona Corporation Commission approved TEP's Certificate of Environmental Compatibility in Jan 2024, construction targeted complete 2026.
properties.status: Marketed by Sun Corridor Inc. as an available business campus for aerospace/advanced-manufacturing tenants; TEP transmission project underway to serve it

properties.name: Kiowa Power Plant-Adjacent Tract (207 ac, US-69)
properties.power_notes: Adjacent to a ~1,250MW natural-gas-fueled electric generating facility (Kiowa Power Plant) south of Kiowa; zoned RA; municipal water utility adjacent to property.
properties.fiber_notes: Two fiber routes to Equinix Dallas (1950 Stemmons); 100Gbps waves, 100Gbps DIA, 100Gbps Ethernet Private Lines cited on listing.
properties.status: Active listing
properties.broker: Listed on Crexi (broker unnamed in listing summary)

properties.name: Kiowa Renewable/Data Center Tract (243 ac, Reynolds Lake Rd)
properties.power_notes: Marketed as ideal for hyperscale data center development; near the same Kiowa Power Plant gas-generation facility as the adjacent 207ac US-69 tract.
properties.status: Active listing

properties.name: Tulsa Port of Catoosa — shovel-ready industrial tracts
properties.power_notes: Public port/industrial park (~2,000 ac total) on the McClellan-Kerr Arkansas River Navigation System; shovel-ready sites 5-50 ac, e.g. Riverview Industrial Park Tract 1 (5.6 ac) and Tract 2 (6.1 ac), "all utilities onsite," priced at $65,000/acre.
properties.status: Available; actively marketed

properties.name: Port Muskogee — available industrial tracts
properties.power_notes: Barge-served industrial port on the McClellan-Kerr Arkansas River Navigation System; ~115 ac available across Tract A (50 ac), Tract D (44 ac), Tract J (18 ac); separate Port Muskogee South parcel (125.49 ac acquired, 81 contiguous riverfront ac outside floodplain, zoned heavy industrial) and John T. Griffin Industrial Park (up to 250 developable ac, Federal Opportunity Zone). No substation/gas-tap specifics published — follow up directly.
properties.status: Available; actively marketed
properties.broker: Port Muskogee, 5201 Three Forks Rd, Fort Gibson, OK 74434, (918) 682-7886

properties.name: MidAmerica Industrial Park (Pryor)
properties.power_notes: ~9,000 ac park (8th-largest industrial park worldwide); over 1,500 available acres, configurable half-acre to 250-acre sites; served by Grand River Dam Authority (GRDA); existing tenants include a Google data center, American Castings, and Pryor Chemical Company — proven powered-land precedent.
properties.status: Available; actively marketed
properties.broker: MidAmerica Industrial Park Administration / Pryor Area Chamber of Commerce

properties.name: USG Southard Gypsum Plant (idling)
properties.power_notes: 100+ year gypsum mine and processing plant; no gas-tap, substation, or rail specifics published yet — flagging for direct follow-up with USG/Blaine County once the facility is vacated.
properties.status: USG announced facility idling and product phase-out targeted fall 2026; ~300 employees affected; not yet vacated/available

properties.name: IREN "Project Emerald" (Kiowa/Pittsburg County)
properties.power_notes: 2,000 ac site (~10% coverage, 90% buffer); ~$50B project; a Pittsburg County committee endorsed a tax-increment district May 2026 but commissioners subsequently tabled the incentive after public pushback; same power-plant corridor as the Kiowa 207ac/243ac listed tracts.
properties.status: Incentive package tabled after public pushback; construction targeted 2027 if approved

properties.name: Google "Project Spring" (Sand Springs)
properties.power_notes: Google committed to fund all new power infrastructure including a dedicated substation, so no costs pass to ratepayers; part of Google's broader $9B Oklahoma cloud/AI investment announced Aug 2025.
properties.status: Sand Springs council approved 6-1 on Feb 3, 2026 (contested locally); construction targeted 2027, completion 2029

properties.name: Meta "Project Anthem" (Fair Oaks Industrial Park, East Tulsa)
properties.power_notes: Proposed ~340 ac campus within the larger 2,000-ac Fair Oaks industrial innovation park; Public Service Company of Oklahoma (PSO) named as utility, with Meta funding grid infrastructure including substations/transmission.
properties.status: Groundbreaking reported April 2026; $1B+ investment; completion targeted 2028

properties.name: Project Jupiter (STACK/BorderPlex/Oracle-OpenAI, Santa Teresa)
properties.power_notes: ~1,400 ac; four buildings, microgrid, battery storage, desalination plant; pivoted to Bloom Energy fuel cells (Apr 2026) for cleaner/water-efficient generation. Companion "Green Chile Project" gas pipeline (Transwestern/Energy Transfer, 17.77 mi, 24-inch, connects to El Paso Natural Gas system, 400,000 dekatherms/day) was denied twice by the NM State Land Commissioner (Mar & Jul 2026) for state-trust-land right-of-way, though BLM approved a federal right-of-way in May 2026 — gas-supply status is contested.
properties.status: Under construction/permitting; Doña Ana County approved up to $165B in Industrial Revenue Bonds; air-quality permit hearing scheduled Oct 2026

properties.name: New Era Energy & Digital — Lea County AI/gas/nuclear campus
properties.power_notes: 3,500 ac land option (signed ~Nov 6, 2025); up to 7GW planned total — initial 2GW on-site natural gas plant targeting first power 2028, plus planned 5+GW nuclear (technology TBD). Company states proximity to major gas transmission lines, existing power infrastructure, water supply, and fiber. Plans to lease powered parcels/shell buildings to AI/enterprise tenants.
properties.fiber_notes: Company states high-speed fiber connectivity at the site
properties.status: Land option signed; company also faced a NM civil complaint per a Dec 2025 statement — flag for diligence
properties.broker: New Era Energy & Digital — IR: Jonathan.Paterson@harbor-access.com

properties.name: Zenith Volts Corp — Roswell/Chaves County campus
properties.power_notes: 300 ac initial data-center footprint within an 8,500 ac approved site (incl. 250 ac battery storage); designed for 1.25GW+ via on-site solar, natural-gas backup generators, modular solar-thermal hybrid, and geothermal cooling.
properties.status: Chaves County Commission approved the project; targeting operational by Nov 2027; ~$11.7B claimed capital investment

properties.name: Gallup Energy Logistics Park (GELP)
properties.power_notes: 2,500 ac total; 365-ac BNSF-certified rail site (20,000 linear ft rail + 11,000 ft second loop) directly on the BNSF Transcon main line; site literature cites an NM Gas Company pipeline expansion in the area (no tap size stated); currently hosts a $40M crude-oil transload facility.
properties.status: Available; actively marketed by Greater Gallup EDC
properties.broker: Greater Gallup Economic Development Corp — Patricia Lundstrom, (505) 722-2980, patty@gallupedc.com

properties.name: Santa Teresa Gateway Rail Park (Phase 2)
properties.power_notes: Union Pacific-owned 23kV/12.47kV substation on-site with dual transformers; water, sewer, gas, electric, and fiber all available at the facility; adjacent to UP's Santa Teresa Intermodal Complex. Phase 2 cited at 177 ac (145 developable) under NM's Site Readiness Program; broader master-planned park described elsewhere as up to 1,300 ac.
properties.fiber_notes: Fiber available on-site per UP facility description
properties.status: Available; NM Site Readiness Program-designated site
properties.broker: Mesilla Valley Economic Development Alliance — Brian Garcia, VP Business Development, (575) 525-2852, brian@mveda.com

properties.name: Meta Hyperion — expansion to 5GW (Richland Parish)
properties.power_notes: Campus expanded to ~3,650 ac total (original 2,250 + 1,400 ac added quietly end of 2025); planned capacity expanding from 2GW to 5GW (2GW targeted by 2030, full 5GW timeline undisclosed); Meta pledged $1B+ in local infrastructure improvements.
properties.status: Expansion announced; total investment raised to >$50B; construction ongoing (contractors DPR, Turner, Mortenson)

properties.name: Saxum Investments — Northwest Jackson data center rezoning
properties.power_notes: 230 ac rezoning request (expanded from an original 190 ac) on Forest Avenue Extension between Medgar Evers Blvd and I-220, Ashley Acres neighborhood; no tenant named, no power/fiber infrastructure specifics disclosed.
properties.status: Speculative/proposed; Jackson City Council passed a 183-day citywide data-center moratorium (5-2, Jul 14 2026) largely in response to this and other proposals — an airport-area carve-out was exempted
